title: Shared Sources, Tenant Subscriptions, State Machines, and Races · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed incident review reconstructs shared sources, tenant subscriptions, state machines, and races from source and emitted revision, tenant, URL, validator, bytes, runtime validation, Promise settlement, transaction, lease, response, failure, handle cleanup, and recovery evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for idempotent shared-source subscription service after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal deb…
objectives: Register an approved discovered source idempotently from identity, provenance, parser evidence, and current policy.
Create and remove tenant-scoped follows without deleting shared source history and expose explicit desired-state semantics.
Model pending, active, paused, moved, failing, disabled, deleted, and appealed transitions with actors and causes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Feed Ts Source Registration · changed case · Review Feed Ts Source…
steps.instruction:
review case — A review TypeScript team handles case 5915 during a hostile XML and unknown-value admission drill. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile shared sources, tenant subscriptions, lifecycle transitions, authorization, and registration races; browser TypeScript must remain deterministic and free of Node, network, database, and host effects; then change one PostgreSQL constraint, transaction, migration, upsert, query, or search plan and preserve admitted state. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Register an approved discovered source idempotently from identity, provenance, parser evidence, and current policy.

lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Use unique constraints, conflict reads, transaction retry, and equivalent-identity tests to reconcile concurrent registration. Artifact under review: idempotent shared-source subscription service.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, cast one convenient XML object as a feed, omit reader and publisher outcomes, source and emitted revision, discovery and SSRF scope, Atom and namespace cases, entity and dangerous-key limits, unknown runtime validation, Fetch stream and AbortSignal ownership, PostgreSQL client, Promise settlement, tenant, accessibility, recovery, and controlled Node transfer, then accept plausible objects as feed-service proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Feed Ts Registration Race, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Feed Ts Registration Race
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Feed Ts Registration Race: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review TypeScript team handles case 8248 during an inclusive cross-device research reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile shared sources, tenant subscriptions, lifecycle transitions, authorization, and registration races; publisher fetch and retention budgets must remain explicit; then reorder one timer, Promise, claim, lease, fencing, worker, disconnect, or shutdown settlement and exclude stale effects. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Use unique constraints, conflict reads, transaction retry, and equivalent-identity tests to reconcile concurrent registration.

Evidence target: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Feed Ts Registration Race.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Feed aggregation and TypeScript Record what required effort · Review Feed Ts Registration Race, keep Feed Ts Registration Race connected to executable TypeScript unknown-validation, async, PostgreSQL, Express, reader, package, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use unique constraints, conflict reads, transaction retry, and equivalent-identity tests to reconcile concurrent registration.
